questions.q: What was the purpose of the Amsavatarana, the divine descent described in the epic?
options: To reward the gods with earthly pleasures
To relieve the Earth of the burden of demonic kings
To test the devotion of human sages
To restore the Vedas lost in a great flood
questions.explanation: Oppressed by the weight of asuras born as arrogant kings, the Earth begged for relief, and the gods descended in portions to lighten her burden.

questions.q: Krishna is the incarnation of which god?
questions.explanation: Krishna is Vishnu, also called Narayana, born among the Yadavas to relieve the Earth and guide the great war.

questions.q: Draupadi is described as the incarnation of which goddess?
questions.explanation: The fire-born Draupadi was a portion of Shri, the goddess of prosperity, destined to be the wife of the five Pandavas.

questions.q: Which god fathered Yudhishthira?
questions.explanation: Kunti summoned Dharma with her mantra, and the god of righteousness fathered the truthful Yudhishthira.

questions.q: Bhima was the son of which god?
questions.explanation: Bhima was fathered by Vayu, the wind god, which explains his immense strength and storm-like temper.

questions.q: Arjuna was the son of which god?
questions.explanation: Arjuna was born of Indra, king of the gods, and later visited his father's heaven to gather celestial weapons.

questions.q: Which divine pair fathered the twins Nakula and Sahadeva?
options: The Adityas, the sons of Aditi
The Rudras, the attendants of Shiva
questions.explanation: Madri used Kunti's mantra to summon the twin Ashvins, who fathered the handsome twins Nakula and Sahadeva.

questions.q: Karna was born with a portion of which god?
questions.explanation: Karna was the son of Surya, born with golden armor and earrings that marked his solar origin.

questions.q: Bhishma was the incarnation of what kind of celestial being?
options: A gandharva musician of Indra's court
A Vasu cursed to be born among mortals
A naga king of the underworld
A yaksha guardian of hidden treasure
questions.explanation: Bhishma was the eighth Vasu, born to Ganga under a curse and condemned to a long mortal life.

questions.q: According to the epic's list of incarnations, the hundred Kauravas were born from portions of what beings?
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva states that Duryodhana's brothers were rakshasas incarnate, born to play their parts in the Earth's unburdening.

questions.q: Duryodhana was said to be the incarnation of what?
options: Kali, the spirit of the dark age
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva identifies Duryodhana as a portion of Kali, the spirit of strife and the darkest age.

questions.q: Shakuni was identified as the incarnation of what?
options: Dvapara, spirit of the third cosmic age
Kali, spirit of quarrel and ruin
The demon Bakasura in human form
questions.explanation: Shakuni embodied Dvapara, the spirit of the age named for the losing throw of the dice, a fitting soul for the master gambler.

questions.q: Vidura embodied which god, born mortal through a sage's curse?
questions.explanation: Cursed by the sage Mandavya, the god Dharma took birth as the wise Vidura in a serving maid's womb.

questions.q: Drona was born from a portion of which celestial figure?
questions.explanation: The weapons master Drona carried a portion of Brihaspati, the wise preceptor of the gods.

questions.q: Dhrishtadyumna, born from fire to slay Drona, carried a portion of which god?
questions.explanation: Fittingly for one who stepped out of the sacrificial flames, Dhrishtadyumna was a portion of Agni.

questions.q: Balarama, Krishna's elder brother, was the incarnation of which being?
questions.explanation: Balarama was the incarnation of Shesha, the thousand-headed serpent on whom Vishnu rests.

questions.q: Krishna and Arjuna were identified as which ancient pair of seers reborn?
questions.explanation: Krishna was Narayana and Arjuna was Nara, the inseparable ancient rishis reborn to fight side by side.

questions.q: Why were the eight Vasus cursed to be born as Ganga's mortal sons?
options: They mocked the sage Durvasa at a celestial feast
They refused to fight in the war of gods and demons
They stole the sage Vasishtha's divine cow
They spied on Shiva and Parvati on Mount Kailasa
questions.explanation: The Vasus stole Vasishtha's wish-granting cow Nandini, and the furious sage cursed them to mortal birth.

questions.q: Why did Bhishma alone of the eight Vasus have to live out a long mortal life?
options: He volunteered to stay so his brothers could go free
Ganga loved him too dearly to release him
He was the youngest and had to serve the eldest's term
As the chief offender in the theft, he bore the heaviest sentence
questions.explanation: The Vasu Dyaus actually carried out the theft of the cow, so while Ganga freed his seven brothers at birth, he had to endure a full mortal life as Bhishma.

questions.q: In which book of the Mahabharata is the Amsavatarana, the roll-call of incarnations, narrated?
options: The Adi Parva, the book of beginnings
The Bhishma Parva, the book of the grandsire
The Shanti Parva, the book of peace
The Svargarohana Parva, the book of ascent
questions.explanation: The Amsavatarana section of the Adi Parva lists which god, demon, or celestial took birth as each major character.

questions.q: Kamsa, the tyrant of Mathura slain by Krishna, was the rebirth of which demon?
questions.explanation: Kamsa was the asura Kalanemi reborn, an old enemy of Vishnu destined to fall to him again.

questions.q: In the Puranic tradition that continues the epic, Devaki, the mother of Krishna, is held to be the rebirth of whom?
options: The Earth herself in human form
questions.explanation: The Harivamsha and the Puranas identify Devaki with Aditi, mother of the gods; the epic's own incarnation list does not make this identification.

questions.q: Abhimanyu was the incarnation of Varchas, the son of which god?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu was Varchas, the brilliant son of Soma the moon god, lent to the Earth for the great war.

questions.q: What condition did Soma set when he allowed his son Varchas to be born as Abhimanyu?
options: The boy would remain on earth only sixteen years before returning
The boy would never marry a mortal woman
The boy would never be made to fight after sunset
The boy would be raised in Dwarka, far away from war
questions.explanation: Unable to bear a longer separation, Soma decreed that Varchas would return after sixteen years, which is why Abhimanyu died at sixteen.

questions.q: Ashwatthama was described as born from combined portions of which beings?
options: Brahma, Surya, and the two Ashvins
Shiva along with Yama, Kama, and Krodha
Vishnu, Vayu, and the serpent Shesha
Indra, Agni, and the spirit of Kali
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva says Ashwatthama sprang from united portions of Mahadeva, Yama, desire, and wrath, foreshadowing his terrible deeds.

questions.q: Jarasandha, the emperor of Magadha, carried a portion of which demon?
questions.explanation: The mighty Jarasandha was born of a portion of the asura Viprachitti according to the epic's roll of incarnations.

questions.q: Shishupala, slain by Krishna's discus, was the rebirth of which being?
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva names Shishupala as Hiranyakashipu reborn, though Puranic retellings expand this into the Jaya and Vijaya cycle of three demonic births.

questions.q: Crushed by the weight of demonic kings, to whom did the Earth first carry her appeal for relief?
options: Vishnu, asleep on the cosmic ocean
questions.explanation: The Earth appealed to Brahma, who then commanded the gods to take birth in portions among mortals.

questions.q: At Draupadi's wedding, Vyasa told Drupada a secret tale explaining her five husbands. Who were the Pandavas in that account?
options: Five rishis reborn from a single shared penance
The five faces of Shiva given human form
Five former Indras imprisoned by Shiva and reborn
Five Vasus released early from Vasishtha's curse
questions.explanation: Vyasa revealed that five arrogant former Indras had been imprisoned by Shiva and reborn as the Pandavas, with Shri reborn as their common wife.

questions.q: Who recites the Amsavatarana list of divine incarnations, and to whom?
options: Vaishampayana to King Janamejaya at the snake sacrifice
Sanjaya to Dhritarashtra on the eve of the great war
Krishna to Arjuna between the two assembled armies
Vyasa to Ganesha as the epic was first written down
questions.explanation: Like the rest of the epic's main narration, the Amsavatarana is told by Vaishampayana to Janamejaya during the snake sacrifice.
